data.title: Trump, Schumer Near Potential Deal To Avert Government Shutdown: Report
data.description:
President Donald Trump and Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) are reportedly close to a deal that could prevent a potential government shutdown.

Senate Eyes Limits On Immigration Agents

The two leaders are weighing new limits on federal immigration agents, with funding for several agencies set to expire early Saturday, reported The New York Times on Thursday.

Senate leaders plan to split Homeland Security funding from a broader six-bill spending package needed to fund the military, health programs, and other federal agencies. The Senate aims to pass the bills before the Friday midnight deadline and may approve a short-term extension for Homeland Security to avoid disruptions to agencies such as the TSA, Coast Guard, and FEMA.

The bill proposes new limits on immigration enforcement tactics and increases accountability measures for officers accused of using excessive force, aligning with Democratic demands. These include federal agents enforcing Trump's deportation campaign, identifying themselves openly without masks, using body cameras, and ending indiscriminate raids along with arrests or searches carried out without warrants.

The stopgap bill would give lawmakers and the White House additional time to negotiate a new homeland security funding measure.

Trump‑Schumer Talks Follow DHS Funding Standoff

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: What's Going On With Adobe Stock Thursday?
data.description:
Adobe Inc. (NASDAQ:ADBE) shares are trading lower in premarket hours on Thursday. The decline reflects a broader trend as technology stocks are generally lower today, with the sector experiencing a 0.7% loss.

The stock is caught in a broader sell-off, with the Nasdaq down 1.2%, indicating that external market pressures are influencing its performance.

Apple Competition Adds Pressure

Adobe’s weakness comes days after Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) announced Apple Creator Studio on Jan. 13, a new subscription bundle designed for creators across Mac, iPad, and iPhone. The platform combines Final Cut Pro, Logic Pro, Pixelmator Pro, and productivity tools, directly competing with Adobe’s Creative Cloud Pro.

Apple priced the service at $12.99 per month or $129 annually with a one-month trial. Education users can subscribe for $2.99 per month or $29.99 per year. According to The Verge’s report, Adobe’s subscription costs $69.99 per month, while Adobe’s single apps like Photoshop, Premiere, and Illustrator cost $22.99 per month.

Technical Analysis

Currently, Adobe is trading 7.8% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 13.5% below its 100-day SMA, indicating a bearish short-term trend. Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 32.17%, and they are currently positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, reflecting ongoing weakness in the stock.

The RSI is at 34.36, which is considered neutral territory, while the MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock. This combination suggests that while the stock is not in oversold territory, the momentum remains negative.

The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD indicates mixed momentum, suggesting that traders should remain cautious.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Investors are looking ahead to the next earnings report on Mar. 12.

  • EPS Estimate: $5.46 (Up from $5.08 YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $6.28 billion (Up from $5.71 billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 17.9x (Indicates fair valuation)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $432.35. Recent analyst moves include:

  • UBS: Neutral (Lowers Target to $340.00) (Jan. 26)
  • Oppenheimer: Downgraded to Perform (Jan. 13)
  • BMO Capital: Downgraded to Market Perform (Lowers Target to $375.00) (Jan. 9)

Valuation Insight: While the stock trades at a fair P/E multiple, the strong consensus and 7% expected earnings growth suggest analysts view this growth as justification for the 41% upside to analyst targets.

  • Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Benzinga Edge scorecard for Adobe, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Value: Weak (Score: 24.34) — Trading at a steep premium relative to peers.
  • Quality: Neutral (Score: 48.84) — Balance sheet remains healthy.
  • Momentum: Weak (Score: 7.87) — Stock is underperforming the broader market.

ADBE Price Action: Adobe shares were down 2.41% at $292.36 during premarket trading on Thursday. The stock is trading near its 52-week low of $288.33, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by Mats-Wiklund via Shutterstock
